column6:
There were total of 4 sections
Total 9 questions in 61 minutes

Aptitude/Reasons - 3 questions each of 5 Marks

Python - 4 questions

SQL - 1 Question (Coding 50 Marks)

Coding Round - 1 Questions (75 Marks)

Aptitude questions were from coding decoding, number system, logical thinking 

Python - List, dictionary, slicing, and Advanced Decorators.

SQL - I had to write the code to calculate the Aggregate Marks of students who are having marks above or equal to 500. 

Coding question - Scatter Palindrome, suppose input is ["bbrg"] a string is a Scatter Palindrome if it's letters can be rearranged to form a palindrome. Then for bbrg output is 12
It can be rearranged like - b, bb, brb, bbrr, bbgrr, b, rbr, r, rr, rgr, r, g
column7: Panel of 2 members, they asked me about my interest, then questions from Java, Python, Pandas, Web Scraping, Automation, Logical Puzzles (bulb and switches), difference between C++ and Java which is better why not Python, then they asked questions related to my project also about my on going and final year project, challenges, then question from aptitude test, then general HR questions, like what is my hobby, I have been put into a situation where I have to do the repetitive tasks, etc
column8: Web Scraping, Automation, Pandas, Java, Python
